knownIssues.title: XR2 Gen 1 thermal throttling during Link
knownIssues.description: The Quest 2's Snapdragon XR2 Gen 1 can overheat during extended Link sessions, causing the headset to reduce decode performance.
knownIssues.workaround: Keep room temperature below 25°C. Take breaks during extended sessions. A small fan pointed at the headset helps.

knownIssues.title: USB 2.0 fallback on some motherboards
knownIssues.description: Some USB ports on motherboards silently fall back to USB 2.0 speeds, severely limiting Link quality.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a USB 3.0 port directly on the motherboard (rear I/O). Avoid front panel USB ports. Check bandwidth in Oculus PC app.

optimizationTips.tip: Lower render resolution for older GPUs
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: If your GPU is a few years old, reducing the picture quality slightly can make VR much smoother.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Set SteamVR render resolution to 0.8-1.0x for GTX 1060/1070 class GPUs. The Quest 2's lower native resolution (1832x1920) means you need less GPU power than newer headsets. Prioritize 90Hz over higher supersampling.

optimizationTips.tip: Use Virtual Desktop for lowest wireless latency
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Virtual Desktop is generally the fastest way to stream PC VR wirelessly to a Quest 2.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Virtual Desktop's custom encoding pipeline typically achieves 5-10ms lower motion-to-photon latency than Air Link on Quest 2. Enable SSW (Synchronous Spacewarp) in VD settings for smooth 45fps→90fps reprojection.
